[[Category:八幡宮|やまもとはちまんくう]] [[Category:大阪府の神社|やまもとはちまんくう]] [[Category:八尾市|やまもとはちまんくう]]

===山本八幡宮=== [[画像:Yamamoto-Hachimangu.JPG|thumb|right|256px|山本八幡宮社殿]] '''山本八幡宮'''（やまもとはちまんぐう）は、大阪府八尾市山本町一丁目にある[[神社]]。
近鉄河内山本駅のすぐ近く、玉串川の西岸、立石街道の南沿いに所在する。小ぢんまりとした神社である。 祭神は[[誉田別尊]]。すなわち[[応神天皇]]である。 ====歴史・概要==== *享保元年（1716年）、[[石清水八幡宮]]より神霊を勧請し、山本新田の鎮守として分祀された。 *1956年に社殿が改築され、現在に至っている。本殿は社殿の中に収まっている。 *1994年から1997年にかけて社殿を除いたすべての施設に大幅な改修が行われた。 社務所・集会所・神楽殿は撤去され、境内北西角に鉄筋4階建てのビルが建てられた。 さらに敷地東側・玉串川の間に歩道が整備された。（それ以前は川岸ぎりぎりまで神社境内だった。） 正面にあった太鼓橋は撤去された。


[[Category:八幡宮|やまもとはちまんくう]] [[Category:大阪府の神社|やまもとはちまんくう]] [[Category:八尾市|やまもとはちまんくう]]

'''山本八幡宮'''（やまもとはちまんぐう）は、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]][[河内山本|山本町]]にある[[神社]]。 近鉄[[河内山本駅]]のすぐ北側、[[玉串川]]の西岸、立石街道の南沿いに所在する、小ぢんまりとした神社である。
誉田別尊（[[応神天皇]]）を祀る。
==歴史== [[享保]]元年（[[1716年]]）、[[石清水八幡宮]]より神霊を勧請し、[[河内山本|山本新田]]の鎮守として分祀された。
[[1956年]]に社殿が改築され、現在に至っている。本殿は社殿の中に収まっている。
1994年から[[1997年]]にかけて社殿を除いたすべての施設に大幅な改修が行われた。 社務所・集会所・神楽殿は撤去され、境内北西角に鉄筋4階建てのビルが建てられた。 さらに敷地東側・玉串川の間に歩道が整備された。（それ以前は川岸ぎりぎりまで神社境内だった。） 正面にあった太鼓橋は撤去された。

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=山本八幡宮 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[八尾市]][[河内山本|山本町]]1-2-16 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|37|42.6|N|135|37|11|E|region:JP}} |祭神=誉田別尊 |社格=旧[[村社]] |創建=[[享保]]元年（[[1716年]]） |別名=山本八幡神社（旧称） |例祭=夏季大祭（7月14・15日）
秋季大祭（10月14・15日） |画像=[[File:Yamamoto-Hachimangu.JPG|250px|山本八幡宮社殿]]}} '''山本八幡宮'''（やまもとはちまんぐう）は、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]][[河内山本|山本町]]にある[[神社]]。 [[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==概要== 誉田別尊（[[応神天皇]]）を祀る。近鉄[[河内山本駅]]のすぐ北側、[[玉串川]]の西岸、立石街道の南沿いに所在する、小ぢんまりとした神社である。 ==歴史== [[享保]]元年（[[1716年]]）、[[河内山本|山本新田]]の開発者である山中庄兵衛正永と、本山弥右衛門重英（加賀屋）の両人らが[[石清水八幡宮]]より神霊を勧請[http://www.cave-ens.com/pdf/cens7_010.pdf 第 15 代応神天皇、山本八幡宮の由来と鳥居 - 洞窟環境NET学会 紀要7号]し、山本新田の鎮守として分祀された。当初は「山本八幡神社」という名称だった。その後、享保13年 (1728) 正月に加賀屋から泉屋[[住友吉左衛門]]へ所有が変わった後には、住友家の尊崇と保護を受け近代まで至る。 昭和15年（1940年）に住友住宅による山本新田での宅地分譲が終了し、212年に渡る住友家の地主管理が終了する。その際に住友住宅より東南部分の土地を寄贈されている。同年に玉串川沿いにあった池を埋めたて、社務所が新築される。 昭和31年（1956年）に旧[[橿原神宮]]の古材を用いて社殿が改築され、[[恩智神社]]の[[社家]]である新海（にいみ）氏より専任の宮司を迎え入れている。この頃に旧山本新田住友会所の建物の移築もあり境内が整備され、「山本八幡宮」と改称し現在に至っている。本殿は社殿の中に収まっている。 1994年から[[1997年]]にかけて社殿を除いたすべての施設に大幅な改修が行われた。 社務所・集会所・神楽殿は撤去され、境内北西角に鉄筋4階建てのビルが建てられた。 さらに敷地東側・玉串川の間に歩道が整備された{{refnest|group=注釈|以前は川岸ぎりぎりまで神社境内だったが、平成5年（1993年）に八尾市の都市整備計画の一環で川沿い4m幅を遊歩道にする計画があり、神社側がそれに応じて割譲した。}}。正面にあった太鼓橋は撤去された。 ==摂末社== *摂社： 妙見(みょうけん）宮 :祭神は北辰明神(ホクシンミョウジン）。北辰明神は北極星が神格化されたものであり、国土の守護や災害の滅除、長寿延命に霊験があるとされている。かつては住友家の守護神であった。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' *[[大阪府]][[八尾市]][[河内山本|山本町]]1-2-16 '''交通アクセス''' * 最寄駅：[[近鉄大阪線]][[河内山本駅]]下車後、北隣すぐ == 出典 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} == 外部リンク == *[http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html 山本八幡宮 - 大阪府神道庁] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまもとはちまんくう}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:八尾市]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

{{神社 |名称=山本八幡宮 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[八尾市]][[河内山本|山本町]]1-2-16 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|37|42.6|N|135|37|11|E|region:JP}} |祭神=誉田別尊 |社格=旧[[村社]] |創建=[[享保]]元年（[[1716年]]） |別名=山本八幡神社（旧称） |例祭=夏季大祭（7月14・15日）

秋季大祭（10月14・15日） |画像=[[File:Yamamoto-Hachimangu.JPG|250px|山本八幡宮社殿]] }} '''山本八幡宮'''（やまもとはちまんぐう）は、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]][[山本町 (八尾市)|山本町]]にある[[神社]]。 [[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 概要 == 誉田別尊（[[応神天皇]]）を祀る。近鉄[[河内山本駅]]のすぐ北側、[[玉串川]]の西岸、立石街道の南沿いに所在する、小ぢんまりとした神社である。 == 歴史 == [[享保]]元年（[[1716年]]）、[[河内山本|山本新田]]の開発者である山中庄兵衛正永と、本山弥右衛門重英（加賀屋）の両人らが[[石清水八幡宮]]より神霊を勧請[http://www.cave-ens.com/pdf/cens7_010.pdf 第 15 代応神天皇、山本八幡宮の由来と鳥居 - 洞窟環境NET学会 紀要7号]し、山本新田の鎮守として分祀された。当初は「山本八幡神社」という名称だった。その後、享保13年 (1728) 正月に加賀屋から泉屋[[住友吉左衛門]]へ所有が変わった後には、住友家の尊崇と保護を受け近代まで至る。 昭和15年（1940年）に住友住宅による山本新田での宅地分譲が終了し、212年に渡る住友家の地主管理が終了する。その際に住友住宅より東南部分の土地を寄贈されている。同年に玉串川沿いにあった池を埋めたて、社務所が新築される。 昭和31年（1956年）に旧[[橿原神宮]]の古材を用いて社殿が改築され、[[恩智神社]]の[[社家]]である新海（にいみ）氏より専任の宮司を迎え入れている。この頃に旧山本新田住友会所の建物の移築もあり境内が整備され、「山本八幡宮」と改称し現在に至っている。本殿は社殿の中に収まっている。 1994年から[[1997年]]にかけて社殿を除いたすべての施設に大幅な改修が行われた。 社務所・集会所・神楽殿は撤去され、境内北西角に鉄筋4階建てのビルが建てられた。 さらに敷地東側・玉串川の間に歩道が整備された{{refnest|group=注釈|以前は川岸ぎりぎりまで神社境内だったが、平成5年（1993年）に八尾市の都市整備計画の一環で川沿い4m幅を遊歩道にする計画があり、神社側がそれに応じて割譲した。}}。正面にあった太鼓橋は撤去された。 == 摂末社 == *摂社： 妙見(みょうけん）宮 :祭神は北辰明神(ホクシンミョウジン）。北辰明神は北極星が神格化されたものであり、国土の守護や災害の滅除、長寿延命に霊験があるとされている。かつては住友家の守護神であった。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' *[[大阪府]][[八尾市]][[山本町 (八尾市)|山本町]]1-2-16 '''交通アクセス''' * 最寄駅：[[近鉄大阪線]][[河内山本駅]]下車後、北隣すぐ == 出典 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html 山本八幡宮 - 大阪府神道庁] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまもとはちまんくう}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

'''Yamamoto Hachimangū''' (山本八幡宮) refers to several {{ill|Shinto shrines|ja|神社}} of the {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮}} type.
* {{ill|Yamamoto Hachimangū (Fujinomiya City)|ja|山本八幡宮 (富士宮市)}} - Located in {{ill|Fujinomiya City|ja|富士宮市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. * {{ill|Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)|ja|山本八幡宮 (八尾市)}} - Located in {{ill|Yao City|ja|八尾市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.

'''Yamamoto Hachimangū''' (山本八幡宮) refers to several {{ill|Shinto shrines|ja|神社}} of the {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮}} type.
* {{ill|Yamamoto Hachimangū (Fujinomiya City)|ja|山本八幡宮 (富士宮市)}} - Located in {{ill|Fujinomiya City|ja|富士宮市}}, {{ill|Shizuoka Prefecture|ja|静岡県}}. * {{ill|Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)|ja|山本八幡宮 (八尾市)}} - Located in {{ill|Yao City|ja|八尾市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}.
{{Disambiguation}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Yamamoto Hachimangū}} [[Category:Shrines with identical names]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refresh]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Yamamoto Hachimangū]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E6%9C%AC%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE (%E5%85%AB%E5%B0%BE%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:山本八幡宮]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/山本八幡宮]]

{{Shrine |name = Yamamoto Hachimangu (山本八幡宮) |image = [[File:Yamamoto-Hachimangu.JPG|250px|Yamamoto Hachimangu Shrine Hall]] |location = [[Osaka Prefecture|大阪府]], [[Yao, Osaka|八尾市]], [[Yamamotocho, Yao|山本町]] 1-2-16 |position = {{coord|34|37|42.6|N|135|37|11|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deity = Hondawake no Mikoto (誉田別尊, Emperor Ōjin) |shrine_rank = Former [[Mura-sha|村社]] |established = [[Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) |former_name = Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine (山本八幡神社) |main_festivals = Summer Grand Festival (July 14-15)
Autumn Grand Festival (October 14-15) }} '''Yamamoto Hachimangu''' (山本八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yao, Osaka|ja|八尾市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It enshrines Hondawake no Mikoto, also known as Emperor Ōjin, and served as a guardian shrine for the Yamamoto Shinden area during its development. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. ## Overview The shrine venerates Hondawake no Mikoto (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|ja|河内山本駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. ## History Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in [[Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田}} area. They enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangu|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|ja|橿原神宮}}. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. ## Sub-Shrines * **Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)** Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. ## Festivals and Events * **Summer Grand Festival** (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * **Autumn Grand Festival** (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. ## Access * **Nearest Station:** [[Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}} is located immediately north of the shrine. ## References {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} ## External Links * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Osaka Shrine Office] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Hachimangu]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamamoto Hachimangū}} [[Category:Shrines with identical names]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refresh]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Yamamoto Hachimangū]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E6%9C%AC%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE (%E5%85%AB%E5%B0%BE%E5%B8%82)]] [[ja:山本八幡宮]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/山本八幡宮]]

{{Shrine |name = Yamamoto Hachimangu (山本八幡宮) |image = [[File:Yamamoto-Hachimangu.JPG|250px|Yamamoto Hachimangu Shrine Hall]] |location = [[Osaka Prefecture|大阪府]], [[Yao, Osaka|八尾市]], [[Yamamotocho, Yao|山本町]] 1-2-16 |position = {{coord|34|37|42.6|N|135|37|11|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deity = Hondawake no Mikoto (誉田別尊, Emperor Ōjin) |shrine_rank = Former [[Mura-sha|村社]] |established = [[Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) |former_name = Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine (山本八幡神社) |main_festivals = Summer Grand Festival (July 14-15)

Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|ja|橿原神宮}}. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. ## Sub-Shrines * **Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)** Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. {{Shrine |name = Yamamoto Hachimangu (山本八幡宮) |image = [[File:Yamamoto-Hachimangu.JPG|250px|Yamamoto Hachimangu Shrine Hall]] |location = [[Osaka Prefecture|大阪府]], [[Yao, Osaka|八尾市]], [[Yamamotocho, Yao|山本町]] 1-2-16 |position = {{coord|34|37|42.6|N|135|37|11|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deity = Hondawake no Mikoto (誉田別尊, Emperor Ōjin) |shrine_rank = Former [[Mura-sha|村社]] |established = [[Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) |former_name = Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine (山本八幡神社) |main_festivals = Summer Grand Festival (July 14-15)
Autumn Grand Festival (October 14-15) }} '''Yamamoto Hachimangu''' (山本八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yao, Osaka|ja|八尾市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It enshrines Hondawake no Mikoto, also known as Emperor Ōjin, and served as a guardian shrine for the Yamamoto Shinden area during its development. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. ## Overview The shrine venerates Hondawake no Mikoto (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|ja|河内山本駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. ## History Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in [[Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田}} area. They enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangu|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|ja|橿原神宮}}. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. ## Sub-Shrines * **Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)** Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. ## Festivals and Events * **Summer Grand Festival** (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * **Autumn Grand Festival** (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. ## Access * **Nearest Station:** [[Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}} is located immediately north of the shrine. ## References {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} ## External Links * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Osaka Shrine Office] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Hachimangu]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT::Yamamoto Hachimangū}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:defautsort]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refresh]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Yamamoto Hachimangū]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:New pages]][[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E6%9C%AC%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE (%E5%85%AB%E5%B0%BE%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:山本八幡宮]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/山本八幡宮]]

Autumn Grand Festival (October 14-15) }} '''Yamamoto Hachimangu''' (山本八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yao, Osaka|ja|八尾市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It enshrines [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], also known as Emperor Ōjin, and served as a guardian shrine for the Yamamoto Shinden area during its development. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|ja|河内山本駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in [[Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田}} area. They enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangu|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|ja|橿原神宮}}. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. == Sub-Shrines == * '''Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)''' Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' [[Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}} is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Osaka Shrine Office] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Hachimangu]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture shrines]] {{citation needed|Yamamoto Hachimangū}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)}}[[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:refresh]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Yamamoto Hachimangū]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:New pages]][[Category:dd]][[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E6%9C%AC%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE (%E5%85%AB%E5%B0%BE%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] [[ja:山本八幡宮]] [[simple:user:immanuelle/山本八幡宮]]

Autumn Grand Festival (October 14-15) }} '''Yamamoto Hachimangu''' (山本八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yao, Osaka|ja|八尾市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It enshrines [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], also known as Emperor Ōjin, and served as a guardian shrine for the Yamamoto Shinden area during its development. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|ja|河内山本駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in [[Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田}} area. They enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangu|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|ja|橿原神宮}}. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. == Sub-Shrines == * '''Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)''' Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' [[Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}} is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Autumn Grand Festival (October 14-15) }} '''Yamamoto Hachimangu''' (山本八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yao, Osaka|ja|八尾市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It enshrines [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], also known as Emperor Ōjin, and served as a guardian shrine for the Yamamoto Shinden area during its development. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|ja|河内山本駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in [[Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田}} area. They enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangu|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|ja|橿原神宮}}. It enshrines [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], also known as Emperor Ōjin, and served as a guardian shrine for the Yamamoto Shinden area during its development. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|ja|河内山本駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in [[Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田}} area. They enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangu|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|ja|橿原神宮}}. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. == Sub-Shrines == * '''Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)''' Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' [[Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}} is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - {{Shrine |name = Yamamoto Hachimangu (山本八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yamamoto-Hachimangu.JPG|250px|Yamamoto Hachimangu Shrine Hall]] |location = [[Osaka Prefecture|大阪府]], [[Yao, Osaka|八尾市]], [[Yamamotocho, Yao|山本町]] 1-2-16 |position = {{coord|34|37|42.6|N|135|37|11|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deity = [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊, Emperor Ōjin) |shrine_rank = Former [[Mura-sha|村社]] |established = [[Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) |former_name = Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine (山本八幡神社) |main_festivals = Summer Grand Festival (July 14-15)
Autumn Grand Festival (October 14-15) }} '''Yamamoto Hachimangu''' (山本八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yao, Osaka|ja|八尾市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It enshrines [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], also known as Emperor Ōjin, and served as a guardian shrine for the Yamamoto Shinden area during its development. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|ja|河内山本駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in [[Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田}} area. They enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangu|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|ja|橿原神宮}}. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. == Sub-Shrines == * '''Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)''' Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' [[Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}} is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Autumn Grand Festival (October 14-15) }} '''Yamamoto Hachimangu''' (山本八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yao, Osaka|ja|八尾市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It enshrines [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], also known as Emperor Ōjin, and served as a guardian shrine for the Yamamoto Shinden area during its development. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|ja|河内山本駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in [[Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田}} area. They enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangu|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|ja|橿原神宮}}. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. == Sub-Shrines == * '''Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)''' Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. It enshrines [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], also known as Emperor Ōjin, and served as a guardian shrine for the Yamamoto Shinden area during its development. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|ja|河内山本駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in [[Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田}} area. They enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangu|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. {{Shrine |name = Yamamoto Hachimangu (山本八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yamamoto-Hachimangu.JPG|250px|Yamamoto Hachimangu Shrine Hall]] |location = [[Osaka Prefecture|大阪府]], [[Yao, Osaka|八尾市]], [[Yamamotocho, Yao|山本町]] 1-2-16 |position = {{coord|34|37|42.6|N|135|37|11|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deity = [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊, Emperor Ōjin) |shrine_rank = Former [[Mura-sha|村社]] |established = [[Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) |former_name = Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine (山本八幡神社) |main_festivals = Summer Grand Festival (July 14-15)
Autumn Grand Festival (October 14-15) }} '''Yamamoto Hachimangu''' (山本八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yao, Osaka|ja|八尾市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It enshrines [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], also known as Emperor Ōjin, and served as a guardian shrine for the Yamamoto Shinden area during its development. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|ja|河内山本駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in [[Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田}} area. They enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangu|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|ja|橿原神宮}}. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. == Sub-Shrines == * '''Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)''' Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' [[Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}} is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Autumn Grand Festival (October 14-15) }} '''Yamamoto Hachimangu''' (山本八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yao, Osaka|ja|八尾市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It enshrines [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], also known as Emperor Ōjin, and served as a guardian shrine for the Yamamoto Shinden area during its development. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|ja|河内山本駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in [[Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田}} area. They enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangu|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|ja|橿原神宮}}. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. == Sub-Shrines == * '''Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)''' Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. Autumn Grand Festival (October 14-15) }} '''Yamamoto Hachimangu''' (山本八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yao, Osaka|ja|八尾市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It enshrines [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], also known as Emperor Ōjin, and served as a guardian shrine for the Yamamoto Shinden area during its development. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|ja|河内山本駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in [[Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田}} area. They enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangu|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|ja|橿原神宮}}. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. == Sub-Shrines == * '''Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)''' Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. {{Shrine |name = Yamamoto Hachimangu (山本八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yamamoto-Hachimangu.JPG|250px|Yamamoto Hachimangu Shrine Hall]] |location = [[Osaka Prefecture|大阪府]], [[Yao, Osaka|八尾市]], [[Yamamotocho, Yao|山本町]] 1-2-16 |position = {{coord|34|37|42.6|N|135|37|11|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deity = [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊, Emperor Ōjin) |shrine_rank = Former [[Mura-sha|村社]] |established = [[Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) |former_name = Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine (山本八幡神社) |main_festivals = Summer Grand Festival (July 14-15)
Autumn Grand Festival (October 14-15) }} '''Yamamoto Hachimangu''' (山本八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yao, Osaka|ja|八尾市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. It enshrines [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], also known as Emperor Ōjin, and served as a guardian shrine for the Yamamoto Shinden area during its development. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|ja|河内山本駅}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}}, near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in [[Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田}} area. They enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangu|ja|石清水八幡宮}} to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|ja|橿原神宮}}. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. == Sub-Shrines == * '''Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)''' Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' [[Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線}} is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Osaka Shrine Office] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yamamoto Hachimangū}} {{citation needed|Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)}} {{citation needed|Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/山本八幡宮]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Hachimangu]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[Category:refresh]] [[Category:Yamamoto Hachimangū]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E6%9C%AC%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE (%E5%85%AB%E5%B0%BE%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[sn:Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[sn:amamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[ja:山本八幡宮]]

It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' [[Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線|12=draft}} is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Osaka Shrine Office] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yamamoto Hachimangū}} {{citation needed|Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)}} {{citation needed|Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/山本八幡宮]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Hachimangu]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[Category:refresh]] [[Category:Yamamoto Hachimangū]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E6%9C%AC%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE (%E5%85%AB%E5%B0%BE%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[sn:Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[sn:amamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[ja:山本八幡宮]]

{{Shrine |name = Yamamoto Hachimangu (山本八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yamamoto-Hachimangu.JPG|250px|Yamamoto Hachimangu Shrine Hall]] |location = [[Osaka Prefecture|大阪府]], [[Yao, Osaka|八尾市]], [[Yamamotocho, Yao|山本町]] 1-2-16 |position = {{coord|34|37|42.6|N|135|37|11|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deity = [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊, Emperor Ōjin) |shrine_rank = Former [[Mura-sha|村社]] |established = [[Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) |former_name = Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine (山本八幡神社) |main_festivals = Summer Grand Festival (July 14-15)
Autumn Grand Festival (October 14-15) }} '''Yamamoto Hachimangu''' (山本八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yao, Osaka|ja|八尾市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft}}. It enshrines [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], also known as Emperor Ōjin, and served as a guardian shrine for the Yamamoto Shinden area during its development. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|ja|河内山本駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線|12=draft}}, near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川|12=draft}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in [[Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田|12=draft}} area. They enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangu|ja|石清水八幡宮|12=draft}} to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家|12=draft}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|ja|橿原神宮|12=draft}}. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. == Sub-Shrines == * '''Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)''' Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' [[Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線|12=draft}} is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Osaka Shrine Office] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yamamoto Hachimangū}} {{citation needed|Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)}} {{citation needed|Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/山本八幡宮]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Hachimangu]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[Category:refresh]] [[Category:Yamamoto Hachimangū]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E6%9C%AC%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE (%E5%85%AB%E5%B0%BE%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[sn:Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[sn:amamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[ja:山本八幡宮]]

It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' [[Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線|12=draft|12=draft}} is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Osaka Shrine Office] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yamamoto Hachimangū}} {{citation needed|Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)}} {{citation needed|Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/山本八幡宮]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Hachimangu]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[Category:refresh]] [[Category:Yamamoto Hachimangū]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E6%9C%AC%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE (%E5%85%AB%E5%B0%BE%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[sn:Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[sn:amamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[ja:山本八幡宮]]

{{Shrine |name = Yamamoto Hachimangu (山本八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yamamoto-Hachimangu.JPG|250px|Yamamoto Hachimangu Shrine Hall]] |location = [[Osaka Prefecture|大阪府]], [[Yao, Osaka|八尾市]], [[Yamamotocho, Yao|山本町]] 1-2-16 |position = {{coord|34|37|42.6|N|135|37|11|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deity = [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊, Emperor Ōjin) |shrine_Rank = Former [[Mura-sha|村社]] |established = [[Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) |former_name = Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine (山本八幡神社) |main_festivals = Summer Grand Festival (July 14-15)
Autumn Grand Festival (October 14-15) }} '''Yamamoto Hachimangu''' (山本八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Yao, Osaka|ja|八尾市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|12=draft|12=draft}}. It enshrines [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], also known as Emperor Ōjin, and served as a guardian shrine for the Yamamoto Shinden area during its development. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|ja|河内山本駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線|12=draft|12=draft}}, near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川|12=draft|12=draft}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in [[Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田|12=draft|12=draft}} area. They enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangu|ja|石清水八幡宮|12=draft|12=draft}} to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家|12=draft|12=draft}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|ja|橿原神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. == Sub-Shrines == * '''Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)''' Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' [[Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線|12=draft|12=draft}} is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Osaka Shrine Office] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yamamoto Hachimangū}} {{citation needed|Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)}} {{citation needed|Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/山本八幡宮]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Hachimangu]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[Category:refresh]] [[Category:Yamamoto Hachimangū]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E6%9C%AC%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE (%E5%85%AB%E5%B0%BE%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[sn:Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[sn:amamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[ja:山本八幡宮]]

It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' [[Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Osaka Shrine Office] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yamamoto Hachimangū}} {{citation needed|Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)}} {{citation needed|Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)}} [[simple:user:immanuelle/山本八幡宮]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Hachimangu]] [[Category:Osaka Prefecture shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[Category:refresh]] [[Category:Yamamoto Hachimangū]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E6%9C%AC%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE (%E5%85%AB%E5%B0%BE%E5%B8%82)]] [[en:Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[sn:Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[sn:amamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[ja:山本八幡宮]]

revision.comment: 20 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:山本八幡宮 (八尾市) up to rev 100033196
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=山本八幡宮 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[八尾市]][[山本町 (八尾市)|山本町]]1-2-16 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|37|42.6|N|135|37|11|E|region:JP}} |祭神=誉田別尊 |社格=旧[[村社]] |創建=[[享保]]元年（[[1716年]]） |別名=山本八幡神社（旧称） |例祭=夏季大祭（7月14・15日）
秋季大祭（10月14・15日） |画像=[[File:Yamamoto-Hachimangu.JPG|250px|山本八幡宮社殿]] }} '''山本八幡宮'''（やまもとはちまんぐう）は、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]][[山本町 (八尾市)|山本町]]にある[[神社]]。 [[旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 概要 == 誉田別尊（[[応神天皇]]）を祀る。近鉄[[河内山本駅]]のすぐ北側、[[玉串川]]の西岸、立石街道の南沿いに所在する、小ぢんまりとした神社である。 == 歴史 == [[享保]]元年（[[1716年]]）、[[河内山本|山本新田]]の開発者である山中庄兵衛正永と、本山弥右衛門重英（加賀屋）の両人らが[[石清水八幡宮]]より神霊を勧請[http://www.cave-ens.com/pdf/cens7_010.pdf 第 15 代応神天皇、山本八幡宮の由来と鳥居 - 洞窟環境NET学会 紀要7号]し、山本新田の鎮守として分祀された。当初は「山本八幡神社」という名称だった。その後、享保13年 (1728) 正月に加賀屋から泉屋[[住友吉左衛門]]へ所有が変わった後には、住友家の尊崇と保護を受け近代まで至る。 昭和15年（1940年）に住友住宅による山本新田での宅地分譲が終了し、212年に渡る住友家の地主管理が終了する。その際に住友住宅より東南部分の土地を寄贈されている。同年に玉串川沿いにあった池を埋めたて、社務所が新築される。 昭和31年（1956年）に旧[[橿原神宮]]の古材を用いて社殿が改築され、[[恩智神社]]の[[社家]]である新海（にいみ）氏より専任の宮司を迎え入れている。この頃に旧山本新田住友会所の建物の移築もあり境内が整備され、「山本八幡宮」と改称し現在に至っている。本殿は社殿の中に収まっている。 1994年から[[1997年]]にかけて社殿を除いたすべての施設に大幅な改修が行われた。 社務所・集会所・神楽殿は撤去され、境内北西角に鉄筋4階建てのビルが建てられた。 さらに敷地東側・玉串川の間に歩道が整備された{{refnest|group=注釈|以前は川岸ぎりぎりまで神社境内だったが、平成5年（1993年）に八尾市の都市整備計画の一環で川沿い4m幅を遊歩道にする計画があり、神社側がそれに応じて割譲した。}}。正面にあった太鼓橋は撤去された。 == 摂末社 == *摂社： 妙見(みょうけん）宮 :祭神は北辰明神(ホクシンミョウジン）。北辰明神は北極星が神格化されたものであり、国土の守護や災害の滅除、長寿延命に霊験があるとされている。かつては住友家の守護神であった。 == 現地情報 == '''所在地''' *[[大阪府]][[八尾市]][[山本町 (八尾市)|山本町]]1-2-16 '''交通アクセス''' * 最寄駅：[[近鉄大阪線]][[河内山本駅]]下車後、北隣すぐ == 出典 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html 山本八幡宮 - 大阪府神道庁] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまもとはちまんくう}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|ja|橿原神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. == Sub-Shrines == * '''Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)''' Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. {{Shrine |name = Yamamoto Hachimangu (山本八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yamamoto-Hachimangu.JPG|250px|Yamamoto Hachimangu Shrine Hall]] |location = [[Osaka Prefecture|大阪府]], [[Yao, Osaka|八尾市]], [[Yamamotocho, Yao|山本町]] 1-2-16 |position = {{coord|34|37|42.6|N|135|37|11|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deity = [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊, Emperor Ōjin) |shrine_Rank = Former [[Mura-sha|村社]] |established = [[Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) |former_name = Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine (山本八幡神社) |main_festivals = Summer Grand Festival (July 14-15)
Autumn Grand Festival (October 14-15) }} '''Yamamoto Hachimangu''' (山本八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Yao, Osaka|ja|八尾市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It enshrines [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], also known as Emperor Ōjin, and served as a guardian shrine for the Yamamoto Shinden area during its development. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|ja|河内山本駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Osaka Line|ja|近鉄大阪線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in [[Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田|qq=draft|qq=draft}} area. They enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangu|ja|石清水八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|ja|橿原神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. == Sub-Shrines == * '''Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)''' Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. It enshrines [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], also known as Emperor Ōjin, and served as a guardian shrine for the Yamamoto Shinden area during its development. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]], near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagushi River|qq=}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in [[Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Shinden|qq=}} area. They enshrined the deity from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangu]] to protect the newly developed settlement. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former [[:en:Kashihara Shrine|Kashihara Shrine]]. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]], near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagushi River|qq=}}. Autumn Grand Festival (October 14-15) }} '''Yamamoto Hachimangu''' (山本八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], also known as Emperor Ōjin, and served as a guardian shrine for the Yamamoto Shinden area during its development. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]], near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagushi River|qq=}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Shinden|qq=}} area. They enshrined the deity from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangu]] to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo family|qq=}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former [[:en:Kashihara Shrine|Kashihara Shrine]]. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. == Sub-Shrines == * '''Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)''' Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Osaka Shrine Office] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山本八幡宮 (八尾市)|version=100033196|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/山本八幡宮]] [[ja:%E5%B1%B1%E6%9C%AC%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE (%E5%85%AB%E5%B0%BE%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[sn:amamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[ja:山本八幡宮]] [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Yamamoto-hachimangu}} [[ja:山本八幡宮 (八尾市)]]

It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Osaka Shrine Office] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山本八幡宮 (八尾市)|version=100033196|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/山本八幡宮]] [[sn:E5%B1%B1%E6%9C%AC%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE (%E5%85%AB%E5%B0%BE%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[sn:amamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[ja:山本八幡宮]] [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Yamamoto-hachimangu}} [[ja:山本八幡宮 (八尾市)]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

Autumn Grand Festival (October 14-15) }} '''Yamamoto Hachimangu''' (山本八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], also known as Emperor Ōjin, and served as a guardian shrine for the Yamamoto Shinden area during its development. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]], near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagushi River|qq=}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Shinden|qq=}} area. They enshrined the deity from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangu]] to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo family|qq=}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former [[:en:Kashihara Shrine|Kashihara Shrine]]. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. == Sub-Shrines == * '''Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)''' Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Osaka Shrine Office] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山本八幡宮 (八尾市)|version=100033196|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/山本八幡宮]] [[sn:E5%B1%B1%E6%9C%AC%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE (%E5%85%AB%E5%B0%BE%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[sn:amamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[ja:山本八幡宮]] [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Yamamoto-hachimangu}} [[ja:山本八幡宮 (八尾市)]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Shinden|qq=}} area. They enshrined the deity from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangu]] to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo family|qq=}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former [[:en:Kashihara Shrine|Kashihara Shrine]]. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. == Sub-Shrines == * '''Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)''' Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Osaka Shrine Office] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山本八幡宮 (八尾市)|version=100033196|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/山本八幡宮]] [[sn:E5%B1%B1%E6%9C%AC%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE (%E5%85%AB%E5%B0%BE%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[sn:amamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[ja:山本八幡宮]] [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Yamamoto-hachimangu}} [[ja:山本八幡宮 (八尾市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]
