{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11468880]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yamamoto Hachimangu (山本八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yamamoto-Hachimangu.JPG|250px|Yamamoto Hachimangu Shrine Hall]] |location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|大阪府]], [[:en:Yao, Osaka|八尾市]], [[Yamamotocho, Yao|山本町]] 1-2-16 |position = {{coord|34|37|42.6|N|135|37|11|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊, Emperor Ōjin) |shrine_Rank = Former [[Mura-sha|村社]] |established = [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) |former_name = Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine (山本八幡神社) |main_festivals = Summer Grand Festival (July 14-15)
Autumn Grand Festival (October 14-15) }} '''Yamamoto Hachimangu''' (山本八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], also known as Emperor Ōjin, and served as a guardian shrine for the Yamamoto Shinden area during its development. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]], near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagushi River|qq=}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Shinden|qq=|1=Yamamoto Shinden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469095}} area. They enshrined the deity from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangu]] to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo family|qq=|1=Sumitomo family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381758}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former [[:en:Kashihara Shrine|Kashihara Shrine]]. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. == Sub-Shrines == * '''Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)''' Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Osaka Shrine Office] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山本八幡宮 (八尾市)|version=100033196|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[simple:user:immanuelle/山本八幡宮]] [[sn:E5%B1%B1%E6%9C%AC%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE (%E5%85%AB%E5%B0%BE%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[sn:amamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[ja:山本八幡宮]] [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Yamamoto-hachimangu}} [[ja:山本八幡宮 (八尾市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11468880]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:33, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yamamoto Hachimangu (山本八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yamamoto-Hachimangu.JPG|250px|Yamamoto Hachimangu Shrine Hall]] |location = [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|大阪府]], [[:en:Yao, Osaka|八尾市]], [[Yamamotocho, Yao|山本町]] 1-2-16 |position = {{coord|34|37|42.6|N|135|37|11|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊, Emperor Ōjin) |shrine_Rank = Former [[Mura-sha|村社]] |established = [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) |former_name = Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine (山本八幡神社) |main_festivals = Summer Grand Festival (July 14-15)
Autumn Grand Festival (October 14-15) }} '''Yamamoto Hachimangu''' (山本八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], also known as Emperor Ōjin, and served as a guardian shrine for the Yamamoto Shinden area during its development. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]], near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagushi River|qq=}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Shinden|qq=|1=Yamamoto Shinden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469095}} area. They enshrined the deity from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangu]] to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo family|qq=|1=Sumitomo family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381758}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former [[:en:Kashihara Shrine|Kashihara Shrine]]. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. == Sub-Shrines == * '''Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)''' Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Osaka Shrine Office] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山本八幡宮 (八尾市)|version=100033196|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:User:Immanuelle/山本八幡宮]] [[sn:E5%B1%B1%E6%9C%AC%E5%85%AB%E5%B9%A1%E5%AE%AE (%E5%85%AB%E5%B0%BE%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[sn:amamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]] [[ja:山本八幡宮]] [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Yamamoto-hachimangu}} [[ja:山本八幡宮 (八尾市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[en:Yamamoto Hachimangū (Yao City)]]

In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former [[:en:Kashihara Shrine|Kashihara Shrine]]. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. == Sub-Shrines == * '''Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)''' Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Osaka Shrine Office] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山本八幡宮 (八尾市)|version=100033196|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:山本八幡宮]] [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Yamamoto-hachimangu}} [[ja:山本八幡宮 (八尾市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages with former wikipedia userpage links]] {{wikidata link|Q11468880}}

Autumn Grand Festival (October 14-15) }} '''Yamamoto Hachimangu''' (山本八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], also known as Emperor Ōjin, and served as a guardian shrine for the Yamamoto Shinden area during its development. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]], near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagushi River|qq=}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Shinden|qq=|1=Yamamoto Shinden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469095}} area. They enshrined the deity from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangu]] to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo family|qq=|1=Sumitomo family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381758}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former [[:en:Kashihara Shrine|Kashihara Shrine]]. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. == Sub-Shrines == * '''Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)''' Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Osaka Shrine Office] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山本八幡宮 (八尾市)|version=100033196|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:山本八幡宮]] [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Yamamoto-hachimangu}} [[ja:山本八幡宮 (八尾市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:pages with former wikipedia userpage links]] [[Category:Qq 4]] {{wikidata link|Q11468880}}

They enshrined the deity from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangu]] to protect the newly developed settlement. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former [[:en:Kashihara Shrine|Kashihara Shrine]]. It is located just north of [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]], near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagushi River|qq=}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Shinden|1=Yamamoto Shinden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469095}} area. They enshrined the deity from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangu]] to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo family|1=Sumitomo family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381758}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former [[:en:Kashihara Shrine|Kashihara Shrine]]. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. == Sub-Shrines == * '''Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)''' Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Osaka Shrine Office] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山本八幡宮 (八尾市)|version=100033196|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:山本八幡宮]] [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Yamamoto-hachimangu}} [[ja:山本八幡宮 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11468879}}

Autumn Grand Festival (October 14-15) }} '''Yamamoto Hachimangu''' (山本八幡宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yao, Osaka|Yao, Osaka]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. It enshrines [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], also known as Emperor Ōjin, and served as a guardian shrine for the Yamamoto Shinden area during its development. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]], near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagushi River|qq=}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Shinden|1=Yamamoto Shinden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469095}} area. They enshrined the deity from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangu]] to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo family|1=Sumitomo family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381758}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former [[:en:Kashihara Shrine|Kashihara Shrine]]. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. == Sub-Shrines == * '''Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)''' Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Osaka Shrine Office] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山本八幡宮 (八尾市)|version=100033196|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:山本八幡宮]] [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Yamamoto-hachimangu}} [[ja:山本八幡宮 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11468879}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

It is located just north of [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]], near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagushi River|qq=}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Shinden|1=Yamamoto Shinden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469095}} area. They enshrined the deity from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangu]] to protect the newly developed settlement. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former [[:en:Kashihara Shrine|Kashihara Shrine]]. It enshrines [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]], also known as Emperor Ōjin, and served as a guardian shrine for the Yamamoto Shinden area during its development. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. == Overview == The shrine venerates [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Hondawake-no-Mikoto]] (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]], near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagushi River|qq=}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Shinden|1=Yamamoto Shinden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469095}} area. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former [[:en:Kashihara Shrine|Kashihara Shrine]]. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. == Sub-Shrines == * '''Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)''' Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. It is located just north of [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]], near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagushi River|qq=}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Shinden|1=Yamamoto Shinden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469095}} area. They enshrined the deity from [[:en:Iwashimizu Hachimangū|Iwashimizu Hachimangu]] to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo family|1=Sumitomo family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381758}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former [[:en:Kashihara Shrine|Kashihara Shrine]]. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. == Sub-Shrines == * '''Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)''' Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' [[:en:Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station]] on the [[:en:Osaka Line|Kintetsu Osaka Line]] is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Osaka Shrine Office] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山本八幡宮 (八尾市)|version=100033196|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山本八幡宮]] {{Commons category|Yamamoto-hachimangu}} [[ja:山本八幡宮 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11468879}} [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yao, Osaka]]

Autumn Grand Festival (October 14-15) }} '''Yamamoto Hachimangu''' (山本八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Yao, Osaka|en|Yao, Osaka|WD=Q490872|lt=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It enshrines {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}, also known as Emperor Ōjin, and served as a guardian shrine for the Yamamoto Shinden area during its development. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|en|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|WD=Q6379464|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station}} on the {{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|WD=Q1052545|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line}}, near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagushi River|qq=}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō}} 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Shinden|1=Yamamoto Shinden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469095}} area. They enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangu}} to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo family|1=Sumitomo family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381758}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|en|Kashihara Shrine|WD=Q710333|lt=Kashihara Shrine}}. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. == Sub-Shrines == * '''Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)''' Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|en|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|WD=Q6379464|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station}} on the {{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|WD=Q1052545|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line}} is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Osaka Shrine Office] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山本八幡宮 (八尾市)|version=100033196|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山本八幡宮]] {{Commons category|Yamamoto-hachimangu}} [[ja:山本八幡宮 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11468879}} [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Has wikidata]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yao, Osaka]]

It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|en|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|WD=Q6379464|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station}} on the {{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|WD=Q1052545|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line}} is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Osaka Shrine Office] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山本八幡宮 (八尾市)|version=100033196|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山本八幡宮]] {{Commons category|Yamamoto-hachimangu}} [[ja:山本八幡宮 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11468879}} [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yao, Osaka]]

Autumn Grand Festival (October 14-15) }} '''Yamamoto Hachimangu''' (山本八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Yao, Osaka|en|Yao, Osaka|WD=Q490872|lt=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. It enshrines {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}}, also known as Emperor Ōjin, and served as a guardian shrine for the Yamamoto Shinden area during its development. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto}} (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|en|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|WD=Q6379464|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station}} on the {{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|WD=Q1052545|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line}}, near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagushi River|qq=}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō}} 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Shinden|1=Yamamoto Shinden|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469095}} area. They enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangu}} to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo family|1=Sumitomo family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381758}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|en|Kashihara Shrine|WD=Q710333|lt=Kashihara Shrine}}. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. == Sub-Shrines == * '''Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)''' Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|en|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|WD=Q6379464|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station}} on the {{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|WD=Q1052545|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line}} is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Osaka Shrine Office] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山本八幡宮 (八尾市)|version=100033196|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山本八幡宮]] {{Commons category|Yamamoto-hachimangu}} [[ja:山本八幡宮 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11468879}} [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:pages]]

It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|en|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|WD=Q6379464|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|1=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station}} on the {{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|WD=Q1052545|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line|1=Osaka Line}} is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Osaka Shrine Office] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山本八幡宮 (八尾市)|version=100033196|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山本八幡宮]] {{Commons category|Yamamoto-hachimangu}} [[ja:山本八幡宮 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11468879}} [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu]][[ja:山本八幡宮 (八尾市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu]][[ja:山本八幡宮 (八尾市)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu]][[ja:山本八幡宮 (八尾市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yamamoto Hachimangu (山本八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yamamoto-Hachimangu.JPG|250px|Yamamoto Hachimangu Shrine Hall]] |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=大阪府|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Yao, Osaka|en|Yao, Osaka|WD=Q490872|lt=八尾市|1=Yao, Osaka}}, [[Yamamotocho, Yao|山本町]] 1-2-16 |position = {{coord|34|37|42.6|N|135|37|11|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (誉田別尊, Emperor Ōjin) |shrine_Rank = Former [[Mura-sha|村社]] |established = {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō|1=Kyōhō}} 1 (1716) |former_name = Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine (山本八幡神社) |main_festivals = Summer Grand Festival (July 14-15)
Autumn Grand Festival (October 14-15) }} '''Yamamoto Hachimangu''' (山本八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Yao, Osaka|en|Yao, Osaka|WD=Q490872|lt=Yao, Osaka|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It enshrines {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, also known as Emperor Ōjin, and served as a guardian shrine for the Yamamoto Shinden area during its development. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|en|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|WD=Q6379464|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|1=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station}} on the {{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|WD=Q1052545|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line|1=Osaka Line}}, near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagushi River|qq=}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō|1=Kyōhō}} 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Shinden|1=Q11469095|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469095}} area. They enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangu|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo family|1=Sumitomo family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381758}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|en|Kashihara Shrine|WD=Q710333|lt=Kashihara Shrine|1=Kashihara Shrine}}. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. == Sub-Shrines == * '''Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)''' Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|en|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|WD=Q6379464|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|1=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station}} on the {{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|WD=Q1052545|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line|1=Osaka Line}} is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Osaka Shrine Office] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山本八幡宮 (八尾市)|version=100033196|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山本八幡宮]] {{Commons category|Yamamoto-hachimangu}} [[ja:山本八幡宮 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11468879}} [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:pages]]

It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|en|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|WD=Q6379464|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|1=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station}} on the {{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|WD=Q1052545|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line|1=Osaka Line}} is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Osaka Shrine Office] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山本八幡宮 (八尾市)|version=100033196|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山本八幡宮]] {{Commons category|Yamamoto-hachimangu}} [[ja:山本八幡宮 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11468879}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yao, Osaka]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yamamoto Hachimangu (山本八幡宮) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yamamoto-Hachimangu.JPG|250px|Yamamoto Hachimangu Shrine Hall]] |location = {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=大阪府|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Yao, Osaka|en|Yao, Osaka|WD=Q490872|lt=八尾市|1=Yao, Osaka}}, [[Yamamotocho, Yao|山本町]] 1-2-16 |position = {{coord|34|37|42.6|N|135|37|11|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |enshrined_deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (誉田別尊, Emperor Ōjin) |shrine_Rank = Former [[Mura-sha|村社]] |established = {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō|1=Kyōhō}} 1 (1716) |former_name = Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine (山本八幡神社) |main_festivals = Summer Grand Festival (July 14-15)
Autumn Grand Festival (October 14-15) }} '''Yamamoto Hachimangu''' (山本八幡宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Yao, Osaka|en|Yao, Osaka|WD=Q490872|lt=Yao, Osaka|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It enshrines {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, also known as Emperor Ōjin, and served as a guardian shrine for the Yamamoto Shinden area during its development. The shrine's historical ties to the Sumitomo family and its role in local community life make it a significant cultural and spiritual site. == Overview == The shrine venerates {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} (誉田別尊), known as Emperor Ōjin, as its principal deity. It is located just north of {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|en|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|WD=Q6379464|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|1=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station}} on the {{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|WD=Q1052545|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line|1=Osaka Line}}, near the western bank of the {{ill|Tamagushi River|ja|玉串川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagushi River|qq=}}. Despite its relatively small size, the shrine holds historical and cultural significance for the area. == History == Yamamoto Hachimangu was established in {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō|1=Kyōhō}} 1 (1716) by Shōbei Masanaga Yamanaka and Yaemon Shigehide Honzan, who developed the {{ill|Yamamoto Shinden|ja|山本新田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Shinden|1=Q11469095|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11469095}} area. They enshrined the deity from {{ill|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|en|Iwashimizu Hachimangū|WD=Q710098|lt=Iwashimizu Hachimangu|1=Iwashimizu Hachimangū}} to protect the newly developed settlement. Initially called "Yamamoto Hachiman Shrine," the shrine received support and protection from the {{ill|Sumitomo family|ja|住友家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumitomo family|1=Sumitomo family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11381758}}, who managed the lands from Kyōhō 13 (1728) until 1940. During this time, the shrine benefited from land donations and structural improvements under Sumitomo patronage. In 1956, the shrine underwent significant renovations, including the reconstruction of the main shrine building using materials from the former {{ill|Kashihara Shrine|en|Kashihara Shrine|WD=Q710333|lt=Kashihara Shrine|1=Kashihara Shrine}}. The site was further modernized between 1994 and 1997, with new infrastructure and facilities replacing older structures, while the surrounding area was integrated into urban development projects. == Sub-Shrines == * '''Myōken Shrine (妙見宮)''' Enshrines Hokushin Myōjin (北辰明神), the deified North Star. It is associated with protection of the nation, disaster prevention, and longevity, and was historically revered by the Sumitomo family as their guardian deity. == Festivals and Events == * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (July 14-15) A traditional celebration featuring rituals and community activities. * '''Autumn Grand Festival''' (October 14-15) Another major event marked by prayers for harvest and blessings. == Access == * '''Nearest Station:''' {{ill|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|en|Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|WD=Q6379464|lt=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station|1=Kawachi-Yamamoto Station}} on the {{ill|Osaka Line|en|Osaka Line|WD=Q1052545|lt=Kintetsu Osaka Line|1=Osaka Line}} is located immediately north of the shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} === Notes === {{Reflist|group="Note"}} == External Links == * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_02_yao_yamamotohatiman.html Yamamoto Hachimangu - Osaka Shrine Office] {{commons|Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山本八幡宮 (八尾市)|version=100033196|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Yamamoto-hachimangu}} {{wikidata link|Q11468879|co|commons:Category:Yamamoto-hachimangu|ja|山本八幡宮 (八尾市)|ja|山本八幡宮}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:Pages with interwikis with duplicate languages]]
